mechanism_narrative: A positive six-month PAYEMS trend signals expanding household income and resilient corporate revenue expectations; when PAYEMS momentum is positive, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2024-09 PAYEMS vintage value was 159105 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if positive six-month PAYEMS momentum does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-positive momentum in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive policy raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing equity valuation compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns with t-test p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in HOUST greater than 10% signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2024-08 HOUST vintage value was 1356 thousand units
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive mortgage-rate conditions
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contractions below -10% year over year are followed by non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 5% and CPIAUCSL three-month inflation momentum is not declining, sticky inflation prevents policy relief and compresses equity multiples, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is above its prior 3-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate sticky-inflation state produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if interaction terms add no out-of-sample predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month INDPRO growth reversal after prior contraction signals improving production and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses above 0 after being negative in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO growth reversals are not followed by above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered return spread is negative in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth, real consumption demand is expanding rather than reflecting only price inflation, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month growth by at least 0.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:2024-08 RSAFS vintage value was 710773 million dollars
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive real consumption momentum
failure_condition: Reject if real retail-sales momentum triggers do not have higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than nominal retail-sales-only triggers, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with positive PAYEMS momentum identifies a soft-landing labor regime in which income growth persists without recession confirmation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4.2 and PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change > 100 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment positive-payroll state fails to beat unconditional 5-day returns, or if either single proxy fully subsumes the joint signal in walk-forward models.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS from its recent peak while CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is below its six-month average signals credible easing, lowering discount-rate pressure and producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25 percentage points from its 3-month maximum and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is below its 6-month average
regime_hint: early monetary easing cycle after restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if easing-with-disinflation triggers do not generate positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns are not at least 1 percentage point above unconditional 60-day returns.
